heading: Anti-terrorist forfeiture protection
label: 987 Anti-terrorist forfeiture protection
notes: References in TextThe Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, referred to in subsec. (a), are set out in the Appendix to Title 28, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure. The Supplemental Rules for Certain Admiralty and Maritime Claims, which are set out as part of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, were renamed the Supplemental Rules for Admiralty or Maritime Claims and Asset Forfeiture Actions.The Federal Rules of Evidence, referred to in subsec. (b), are set out in the Appendix to Title 28, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.

the innocent owner provisions of , United States Code, apply to the case.section 983(d) of title 18

children.heading: .—Evidence
children.label: b .—Evidence
children.text: In considering a claim filed under this section, a court may admit evidence that is otherwise inadmissible under the Federal Rules of Evidence, if the court determines that the evidence is reliable, and that compliance with the Federal Rules of Evidence may jeopardize the national security interests of the United States.

children.label: C
children.text: subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5, United States Code (commonly known as the “Administrative Procedure Act”).

children.heading: .—Savings clause
children.label: 2 .—Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this section shall limit or otherwise affect any other remedies that may be available to an owner of property under , United States Code, or any other provision of law.section 983 of title 18
